rubric: Hack-a-Sprint 2026 Inkbox rubric (same criteria as scripts/ai-evaluate-submissions.ts)

evaluations.aiReasoning: Strong Inkbox email workflow for clinical notes with structured extraction and thread-level merging—clear real-world use and meaningful SDK depth. README-quality signal is good from the submission text; Loom not reviewed here.

End-to-end support desk with Firestore plus Inkbox-powered email routing and a grounded reply path for low-priority tickets; deployed demo shows completeness. Among the deepest Inkbox integrations in the set.
Repo review: Python agent uses Inkbox for daily JSearch job digests and iter_unread_emails with Groq-powered recruiter replies in-thread—real autonomous email loop despite a sparse README.

Repo review: OutreachDesk app.py uses the Inkbox Python SDK for email-native outreach with governance rules, tests, and structured drafts—empty top-level README hid this; score reflects verified code, not submission text alone.

Creative workaround for Reddit (extension + local server) with Inkbox email delivery of summaries—clear action-oriented agent. Depends heavily on external Claude for analysis; Inkbox is primarily outbound comms.
Daily digest with scraping, summarization, and subscriber flow through Inkbox email including a public agent address—simple, shippable, and on-brief. Deployed URL strengthens completeness.
Description reads as a generic services marketplace (Next.js/Prisma/Supabase) with no Inkbox agent, email, phone, or vault story—does not meet the sprint’s core constraint as written.
Market validation toolkit explicitly centers Inkbox outbound email, queueing, and ethics guardrails—good depth and originality for a 2.5h sprint. Local-first SQLite aligns with a credible demo path.
Ambitious social/agent product description but no Inkbox SDK integration called out; likely off-scope for this hackathon unless the repo adds email/phone/vault agent behavior.
CRM automation agent is on-theme for productivity, but the submission is thin on how Inkbox capabilities are used end-to-end. Score reflects limited evidence in the JSON description.
Placeholder description and template Loom URL; repo points at cursor-boston fork rather than a distinct agent build—minimal demonstrable Inkbox work from the submission package.
LinkedIn outreach automation is relevant to GTM workflows, but the write-up does not establish Inkbox email/phone/vault usage. Penalized for missing Inkbox evidence in the submission text.

PDF annotation pipeline delivered entirely through email using Inkbox plus a clear multi-pass doc flow—tight product story and strong SDK fit. One of the clearest ‘agent does real work’ demos described.
CLI that ingests Inkbox inbox and emails a structured briefing via Gemini—solid email-centric integration with an honest constraint note on SMS. Practical personal productivity angle.

Broad operator console with multiple channels and two Inkbox identities; high complexity for sprint length and strong alignment with ‘agent takes action’ over passive UI. Some scope risk but impressive on-paper depth.
Critical alerts bridged from email webhooks to outbound voice via Inkbox—excellent use of phone capability for an incident workflow. Narrow, high-signal use case.
Interesting growth/automation idea for X/Twitter, but description emphasizes Claude Code and browser tooling without an Inkbox agent story—weak challenge fit as submitted.
Personalized news briefing with RAG, cron delivery, and an Inkbox identity for morning email—rich stack and clear autonomous loop. Localhost deploy URL is a minor completeness ding only.

Fleet daily briefing with structured logs, HTML email, and optional phone briefings via Inkbox—uses email, phone, and vault together in a concrete ops loop. Strong match to the sprint’s multi-capability agent bar (submission JSON omits loom; use --accept-all to write).

Newsletter-as-knowledge-base is interesting for dev content, but the submitted JSON lacks a Loom URL and the description does not spell out Inkbox email/phone/vault behavior—scored conservatively on available evidence (use --accept-all to write).

README and layout show a serious outbound-sales agent: website/careers research, OpenAI draft generation, CSV review workflow, and Inkbox delivery with a safe default to a review inbox and explicit --live gating. Strong email-centric Inkbox fit for a governed GTM workflow.

Node service uses @inkbox/sdk end-to-end: verified webhooks, strict JSON transaction payloads, financial analysis, and threaded replies via messages.send. README documents the pipeline clearly—tight, observable email agent without extra UI surface.

Medical check-in flow combines Inkbox email plus live phone (STT/TTS) with Groq over WebSocket—matches the sprint’s multi-channel agent bar. README is concise; stack is credible for a short sprint (Flask, call_server, bot.py).

ConNET README claims full lifecycle on Email, Vault, and Phone: outbound research + send, inbound classification, vault-backed CRM, agent-to-agent mode, follow-ups, smart intros, plus Flask SSE dashboard and D3 network graph. High breadth and depth versus typical single-feature demos; score reflects documented scope (Loom not reviewed here).
